title: 20 Pounds

obverse.description:
Daniel O'Connell (Lord Mayor of Dublin 1841-1842).
Derrynane Abbey, County Kerry in background.
obverse.lettering:
Banc Ceannais na hÉireann  
Fiche Punt
obverse.lettering_translation:
Central Bank of Ireland
Twenty Pounds

reverse.description:
The Four Courts, Dublin.
A pledge signed in 1845 by a number of early Irish statesmen in the background.
reverse.lettering:
Central Bank of Ireland
£20
Twenty Pounds

comments:
The brick detail in the building is actually '£20' printed repeatedly.
Before 23.9.1997, prefixes contained letters A-L. Starting that date, prefixes contained letters M-Z, excluding O and Q.

 

Visit www.irishpapermoney.com for background information on the 20 Pound design, and also for a full list of dates of issue for the Irish Series C banknotes, with an image of each date, and of all known replacement notes.


An introduction to the Series C, Irish Legal Tender Notes is also on the same web site. Background information on the note issues and images of other dates and denominations and varieties of the notes, as well as Specimen notes are illustrated.
